stories.title: Easter Sunday 2026 — What's Happening in Colorado Springs Today (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Happy Easter, COS families. ViewHouse brunch 9 AM–3 PM ($54.95 adults, $14.95 kids 6–12) — live bunnies + egg hunt. City-wide hunt all day. Layer up — still chilly this morning. 🐣 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: COS got 15 inches of snow four days ago. Today it's Easter Sunday. Highs near 56°F but mornings are still near freezing. Pack layers for outdoor egg hunts and parks. Spring at 6,035 feet. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Happy Easter, Colorado Springs. Here's what's happening for families TODAY.

ViewHouse is hosting a lavish Easter brunch buffet from 9 AM to 3 PM this morning — think photos with live bunnies, Easter egg hunt on the green, and photo stations throughout. Pricing is $54.95 for adults and $14.95 for kids ages 6–12. It's a pretty special way to do Easter brunch if you haven't made it to church yet.

Over at Pamela's Restaurant at Hotel Polaris, they're running face painting, an Easter egg hunt, and a "Paint Your Own Airplane" activity for kids — a nod to the Academy's history. Fun combination.

And a city-wide Easter egg hunt is running all day today — 8 AM to 7 PM — at safe public locations around Colorado Springs. Good option if you want something flexible and free.

One practical note: COS got about 15 inches of snow earlier this week. It's been melting fast, but this morning's lows were still near freezing, and highs today are only around 56°F. If you're heading to any outdoor egg hunts or parks, bundle the kids up — a light jacket at minimum, a heavier layer for early morning.

What's your Easter Sunday plan? 🐣
```

#### Image Caption -- 8:00 AM MT

```
Easter Sunday is here, Colorado Springs. Egg hunts, bunny brunch, and spring sunshine — even if it's a little chilly. Grab your layers and get out there. 🐣
```

---

article.headline: Easter Sunday 2026 in Colorado Springs: Brunches, Egg Hunts, and What Families Need to Know Today

stories.title: CPSC Urgent — Baby Products Recalled from Amazon, TikTok Shop & Shein (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: RECALL ALERT: 4 baby products on Amazon, TikTok Shop & Shein — stop use immediately. Bassinets (strangulation), infant walkers (entrapment), baby gyms (suffocation), toy sets (button cell batteries). #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Bought a baby gym on TikTok Shop or toys from Shein? Check CPSC.gov now. Multiple products with button cell battery hazards + suffocation risks recalled this week. Death-risk designation on several. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
COS parents — four more children's products were recalled for serious safety hazards, and they were all sold through Amazon, TikTok Shop, or Shein. Check your home today.

🔴 Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinets (sold on Amazon, around $40, about 250 units sold): Recalled for strangulation and fall hazards. Violates the mandatory standard for infant sleep products. Stop use immediately.

🔴 Uuoeebb Infant Walkers (sold by BaoD on Amazon): These walkers can pass through standard doorways and fail to stop at step edges. The leg openings are large enough for a child to slip through until their head becomes entrapped. Stop use immediately.

🔴 Mikario Trading Convertible Baby Gyms (sold on TikTok Shop): These 5-in-1 activity gyms that convert to tummy-time mats have been recalled for suffocation hazard. Violates the mandatory standard for infant support cushions. Stop use immediately.

🔴 Cubimana Building Toy Sets (sold by Vatos Toys on Shein): The button cell batteries are accessible to children. If a child swallows one, the resulting internal chemical burns can be fatal. Stop use; remove and safely dispose of the batteries; contact Vatos Toys for a refund.

All four of these products carry death-risk designations from the CPSC. The full recall list with contact information is at CPSC.gov/Recalls.

If you bought any of these — or if you share this with a parent friend who might have — you could be preventing a tragedy. Which online marketplace do you shop most for baby/kids gear?
```

#### Image Caption -- 10:00 AM MT

```
Four urgent recalls on children's products sold through Amazon, TikTok Shop, and Shein. If you have any of these at home, stop use immediately. Details at CPSC.gov.
```

---

article.headline: Four Baby and Children's Products Recalled from Amazon, TikTok Shop, and Shein — Stop Use Immediately

stories.title: D11 Invests $1 Million in Tutoring for Struggling Schools (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: D11 invested $1M in tutoring for struggling schools this spring. If your student is at a school flagged by state accountability data, new academic support may already be available. Ask your school what's offered. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Here's some local education news that doesn't get enough attention.

Colorado Springs School District 11 approved a $1 million investment earlier this year specifically to expand tutoring and academic support at schools that state accountability data flagged as struggling. The money was transferred in early 2026, and implementation is underway this spring semester.

D11 is the largest school district in Colorado Springs and serves families across central COS. The state accountability data driving this investment identifies schools where students are behind grade-level benchmarks — typically in reading and math.

What does this mean for D11 families? If your student attends a school that's been on the state's accountability watch list, new tutoring resources may now be available or expanding this semester. The best move is to contact your school directly to ask what's available and how to sign your student up.

This kind of investment doesn't solve every challenge in a district, but it does signal that leadership is putting money toward the places where it's needed most.

D11 parents — has your school communicated anything about new academic support resources this spring?
```

#### Image Caption -- 3:00 PM MT

```
D11 approved a $1 million investment to expand tutoring at struggling schools — being deployed this spring. If your student is in D11, it's worth asking your school what's available.
```

---

article.headline: D11 Invests $1 Million in Tutoring and Academic Support for Struggling Schools This Spring

stories.title: Spring Outdoor Guide — Garden of the Gods and COS City Programs (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: COS spring outdoor guide: Garden of the Gods free daily, guided walks $5/person, Junior Rangers. City Parks programs: April 16 (ages 3–5), April 23 (homeschool, $4/person), April 24 Fountain Creek hike (grades 3–6). #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Spring in Colorado Springs is here (yes, even with last week's snow). And if you're looking for outdoor family activities in April, this is your guide.

Garden of the Gods is open 5 AM to 9 PM every day — always free, no parking fees. The Visitor and Nature Center is open 9 AM to 5 PM. Daily guided nature walks run at just $5 per person and cover topics like spring wildflowers, geology, local wildlife, and the history of the formations. Junior Ranger Programs run daily at no charge. If you've never taken your kids through the Geo-Trekker Theater ("How Did Those Red Rocks Get There?"), it's worth stopping in.

Cheyenne Mountain State Park is a 10-minute drive from downtown with over 28 miles of trails for all skill levels and 61 campsites if you want an easy overnight with the kids. They've got a playground on-site, coin showers, and laundry. It's also right next to the zoo, which makes for a great two-stop day.

For structured programming through Colorado Springs Parks, Recreation and Cultural Services:

📅 April 16 — Little Learners: Pikes Peak Wonders Series (ages 3–5 with caregiver)
📅 April 23 — Homeschool Programs (kids 8+ can be dropped off; $4/person with prepaid registration)
📅 April 24 — Fountain Creek Regional Park Habitat Exploration (grades 3–6, pond study + hike)

Registration is through the Colorado Springs PRCS portal at coloradosprings.gov/PRCS.

What's your go-to outdoor spot in COS with the kids?
```

#### Image Caption -- 5:00 PM MT

```
Spring is here, COS. Garden of the Gods is free, Cheyenne Mountain State Park is 10 minutes away, and city programs run through April. Here's what to put on the calendar.
```

---

article.headline: COS Spring Outdoor Guide: Garden of the Gods, Cheyenne Mountain, and City Park Programs in April

stories.title: Late-Season Snow Recovery — What COS Families Need for Outdoor Easter (Tier 3)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: April in COS: 15 inches of snow on April 1, sunny Easter Sunday on April 5. That's 6,035 feet for you. Good news: above-normal temps and below-normal precip forecast for the rest of the month. Spring is winning. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Let's talk about this week in Colorado Springs weather — because it was a lot.

We started the week with about 15 inches of snow on April 1st. Then temperatures climbed. Then it was Easter Sunday with sunshine and 50-degree highs. Welcome to spring at 6,035 feet.

Here's the practical reality for outdoor-minded families right now: mornings are still cold. Last night's low was close to 28°F. If you're heading to an Easter egg hunt or taking kids to the park this afternoon, morning layers matter — the temperature swings of 20–25 degrees between sunrise and midday are real.

The good news? The broader outlook for Colorado Springs in April is actually positive. The National Weather Service and long-range forecasters are calling for above-normal temperatures and below-normal precipitation for the east side of Colorado through April and into May. We should see more days like today than like Tuesday.

A few practical tips for spring hiking and outdoor activities in April:
- Check trail conditions before heading out — snowy or muddy trails can be hazardous with young kids
- AllTrails and the City of Colorado Springs parks pages usually have recent condition notes
- Sun protection still matters even on cold days — the elevation means UV exposure even at 50°F
- COS averages about 10 hours of sunshine per day in April — plenty of days to work with

The snow was wild this week. But the calendar says spring, and the forecast agrees. 

What's your favorite post-snow spring activity in Colorado Springs?
```

#### Image Caption -- 12:00 PM MT

```
15 inches of snow on Tuesday. Easter Sunday sunshine on Sunday. That's April in Colorado Springs. Layers out, hope on — spring is winning. 🌷
```

article.headline: 15 Inches of Snow, Then Easter: What Colorado Springs Families Need to Know About April Weather
